release_profiles.purpose: Minimal shipping package for the current Windows-first observe-only and dry-run-first runtime.
notes: This is the recommended default production package.
It excludes diagnostics, tests, docs payloads, and optional live-input support paths.

release_profiles.purpose: Optional ship-adjacent profile for controlled rollout of support modules, replay support, AI boundary layers, and explicitly gated safe-action scaffolding.
notes: Do not make this the default install profile.
Only include it when there is a concrete operational need.

release_profiles.purpose: Separate diagnostic and compatibility bundle for support/debug scenarios.
notes: Must stay separate from the default production package.

release_profiles.purpose: Full repository checkout for development, CI, tests, and hygiene work.

production_include_set.rationale: Current observe-only production runtime, semantic pipeline, verification/recovery path, dry-run path, and primary Windows capture runtime.

runtime_support_set.rationale: Useful for staged rollout, replay, structured AI scaffolding, and narrow explicitly gated safe-action support, but not required in the smallest production package.

diagnostic_only_set.rationale: Compatibility and foreground/diagnostic capture paths that should remain available for debugging but should not ship in the default production package.

dev_test_only_set.rationale: CI, test, local scaffolding, and temporary artifacts; never part of a production package.

archive_docs_only_set.rationale: Engineering and operator documentation that should remain in the repo but not in the runtime artifact.

exclude_from_production_set.rationale: Areas that should stay outside the default production package until there is a real runtime need.

notes: Use explicit include manifests for future release packaging.
Do not derive production contents by subtracting tests from the full repository.

release_smoke_checklist: Confirm the package contents are allowlist-based and exclude tests, docs, temporary artifacts, and diagnostic capture files.
Run an import smoke for the intended production profile.
Confirm production capture policy still rejects diagnostic-only fallback in production mode.
Confirm the semantic pipeline still emits non-actionable candidates by default.
Confirm dry-run and tool-boundary paths remain non-executing by default.
If the optional safe-action extension is present, confirm it remains explicitly gated and disabled by default.
Confirm no archive/docs payload is present in the built runtime artifact.
Confirm no temporary SQLite artifacts or scratch files are present in the release payload.
packaging_considerations: Treat production pruning as profile-based packaging, not repository deletion.
Keep package __init__.py files when required for import stability even if they are excluded from default AI context packs.
Keep diagnostic_bundle separate from the default install path.
Only promote support or diagnostic modules into the default package when there is a concrete operational need.
follow_up_order: Define a concrete packaging manifest for production_base_observe_runtime.
Define an optional manifest for production_runtime_support.
Keep diagnostic capture files in a separate non-default bundle.
Remove .tmp_test_artifacts from any future build context.
Revisit src/universal_visual_os_agent/memory only after it becomes part of a real runtime path.
